role: Senior Engagement Manager — AI & Cloud Transformation
subtitle: AI & Cloud Transformation · Consulting Delivery
hero_sub: 15 years of client-facing consulting delivery — from enterprise AI platform adoption to cloud transformation and digital commerce. I own the engagement lifecycle from requirements to retrospective, maintain executive relationships under pressure, and build the delivery structure that moves complex programs from concept to measurable outcome.

the_case.need: 8+ years managing large complex enterprise programs as a consultant
the_case.answer: 15+ years of external consulting delivery — Eptura (Agile PMO lead, ~10 distributed teams), Understood.org (stabilized a distressed multi-year program, 12+ countries), Crocs (release governance for a public retailer, SOX-compliant), a stealth-mode AI startup (AI pipeline delivery, C-Suite stakeholder management).
the_case.detail: Every recent engagement has been external consulting, not internal employment — the operating model 66degrees runs on. At Eptura I was the client-side Agile PMO lead across ~10 distributed teams spanning India and California. At Understood.org I was brought in to stabilize a distressed, multi-year replatform spanning 12+ countries. At Crocs I owned release governance for a publicly traded retailer under live audit review. Each engagement required arriving fast, building trust, and leaving a measurably improved delivery operation.

the_case.answer: Engagement lead at a stealth-mode AI startup building fine-tuned models (Gemini, GPT-4, Claude via API); OVRSTCH AI-first platform delivery from concept to production; daily hands-on Claude Code practitioner since 2022; 6 production AI-assisted applications shipped in 2026.
the_case.detail: At the stealth AI startup, I owned delivery for a team building fine-tuned models against proprietary datasets — translating ambiguous research objectives into sprint-ready backlogs and measurable KPIs. At OVRSTCH, the AI-native commerce platform I founded, I embedded Claude and ChatGPT into daily operational decisions. This year I shipped 6 production applications using Claude Code as the AI coding agent — AI transformation isn't theoretical for me, it's the current operating mode.

the_case.need: Prior consulting experience and executive communication — required
the_case.answer: All primary recent engagements were as an external consultant: Eptura (Operations Board reporting, ~10 teams), Crocs (retailer leadership, go/no-go cadence), the stealth AI startup (C-Suite daily coordination), Understood.org (VP of Engineering primary contact, advised on PMO structure).
the_case.detail: Executive communication has been a constant across every engagement — not a skill I'm claiming abstractly. At Eptura I produced recurring Operations Board reporting for senior leadership. At Understood.org I was the primary point of contact for the VP of Engineering. At the stealth AI startup I coordinated daily with C-Suite stakeholders on sequencing and priorities. Consulting means earning trust fast — that's the muscle I bring.

the_case.need: Scrum/Agile certification, SAFe, enterprise delivery frameworks
the_case.answer: CSM certified (current). SAFe/PI Planning at DigitasLBI/SunTrust (2014) and Eptura (2022–2023). Scrum-of-Scrums across ~10 teams. Agile transformation at A&E, Underline Communications, and Eptura. Hybrid waterfall/Agile delivery throughout.
the_case.detail: SAFe and PI-aligned delivery is not a single engagement — it spans a decade, from the SunTrust financial-services program at DigitasLBI in 2014 to the ~10-team Eptura PMO in 2022–2023. Scrum-of-Scrums, PI planning, and hybrid delivery frameworks have been the default operating structure, not a one-time certification exercise.

the_case.need: Project scope, finances, schedule, and stakeholder management
the_case.answer: Full engagement ownership across all consulting roles: requirements gathering, scope definition, milestone planning, budget and resource tracking, RAID logs, escalation paths, and executive reporting from kickoff through retrospective.
the_case.detail: Every consulting engagement required owning the full lifecycle — not just facilitation. Scope, schedule, and stakeholder management were my responsibility from the first requirements conversation through the final retrospective, with RAID logs and escalation paths maintained throughout as the connective tissue between delivery teams and executive sponsors.

the_case.answer: Azure/Kubernetes delivery at Eptura, Crocs, and Understood.org. Hands-on AI tooling fluency (Claude, GPT-4, Jira AI, Copilot). Front-end engineering background (HTML, PHP, JavaScript, CSS) from career foundation. 6 production applications shipped in 2026 using AI-assisted development.
the_case.detail: The technical fluency is not borrowed. I started as a front-end engineer coding HTML, PHP, JavaScript, and CSS, and this year returned to hands-on building — 6 production applications across audio DSP, 3D graphics, and secure web architecture using Claude Code. Layer that on top of Azure/Kubernetes delivery experience at three enterprise engagements, and I can read a developer's code, understand a deployment pipeline, and translate a technical constraint for an executive without losing anything in translation.

the_case.need: Cross-functional engagement: cloud, data, AI, application development, digital experience
the_case.answer: Led AI data/automation (stealth AI startup), cloud SaaS delivery (Eptura), digital platform transformation (Understood.org, A&E SVOD), eCommerce (Crocs, OVRSTCH), and media/streaming (A&E, Meta).
the_case.detail: Cross-functional breadth spans nearly every domain 66degrees clients operate in: AI/data (stealth AI startup), cloud SaaS (Eptura), digital platform transformation (Understood.org, A&E), eCommerce (Crocs, OVRSTCH), and media/streaming (A&E, Meta). I've never been a single-domain specialist — the engagement changes, the delivery discipline doesn't.

the_stack.headline: The AI transformation toolkit.
the_stack.subhead: Tools I work in daily or have direct delivery experience with.

nodes.label: Jira & Jira AI
nodes.desc: Administration-level Jira fluency — epics, stories, dashboards, workflows, and AI-assisted reporting
nodes.pm_role: Standardized at Eptura PMO across ~10 teams. Embedded Jira AI at Crocs for a ~30% administrative overhead reduction. Configured SOX-compliant SOD controls directly in Jira workflow.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps & Cloud
nodes.desc: Azure/Kubernetes delivery coordination across enterprise SaaS and retail environments
nodes.pm_role: Delivered on Azure/Kubernetes infrastructure at Eptura, Crocs, and Understood.org — coordinating release readiness and cross-team dependencies, not infrastructure administration.

nodes.label: Claude Code & AI Tools
nodes.desc: Claude AI/Claude Code, ChatGPT/GPT-4, Jira AI, Microsoft Copilot — daily-use AI-assisted delivery
nodes.pm_role: Daily practitioner since 2022. 6 production apps shipped via Claude Code in 2026. Led AI model delivery (Gemini, GPT-4, Claude) at a stealth-mode startup.

nodes.label: Confluence, Smartsheet, Asana
nodes.desc: Documentation, reporting, and cross-team coordination platforms
nodes.pm_role: Used across every consulting engagement for executive reporting, RAID logs, and stakeholder documentation.

nodes.label: GitHub Actions & CMS Platforms
nodes.desc: CI/CD pipelines, WordPress/WooCommerce, Contentful — build and migration coordination
nodes.pm_role: Owned CI/CD pipelines for 6 production apps. Coordinated a Sitecore-to-Contentful replatform at Understood.org through QA and launch.

when_things_break.scenario: AI initiative stalls in proof-of-concept — can't cross into production delivery
response: Diagnose the real bottleneck first — at the stealth AI startup, it was requirements ambiguity, not a technical blocker
Run structured requirements elicitation sessions with research/data science leads to surface testable acceptance criteria
Translate outputs into sprint-ready user stories with measurable KPIs, not vague research goals
Establish a delivery cadence so the team stops rebuilding and starts shipping against a plan
Result: data processing throughput improved ~30% and delivery predictability stabilized within two sprints

when_things_break.scenario: Client executive loses confidence mid-engagement due to missed milestones and unclear status
response: At Understood.org, the program was already distressed when I arrived — milestones had slipped and executive trust was low
Establish daily executive syncs immediately, before any other process fix
Produce a clear milestone recovery plan with a visible burndown the client can see, not just hear about
Rebuild reporting transparency so the next status update never comes as a surprise
Result: delivered the replatform on time with no critical regressions

when_things_break.scenario: Cross-team dependency deadlock blocks delivery across 10+ distributed teams
response: At Eptura, cross-team dependencies were surfacing late and blocking sprint completion
Establish a Scrum-of-Scrums cadence as the primary dependency resolution layer, not an escalation of last resort
Build a Jira dashboard giving leadership real-time dependency health visibility
Resolve blocked items at the SoS level rather than escalating every issue to senior leadership
Result: sprint carryover dropped ~20% over the following quarter

how_i_run_a_room: Weekly engagement status report: milestone progress, risks, blockers, next-period commitments — delivered before the client asks
RAID log maintained and reviewed at every stakeholder touchpoint; risks named before they become issues
Retrospectives produce measurable process changes — not just action lists that expire
Executive escalations always include: the issue, the impact, the proposed resolution, and a timeline
Offshore/distributed team alignment: async standup + next-day context brief so no development day is lost
Go/no-go criteria defined before the sprint, not after the sprint fails

points: Owned release governance cadence — readiness checkpoints, go/no-go decisions, CAB-style change approval
Designed SOX-compliant Segregation of Duties controls embedded directly in Jira workflow
Embedded Jira AI and Microsoft Copilot — ~30% administrative overhead reduction
Protected an on-time go-live with clean post-launch stabilization
Owned end-to-end delivery of AI data and automation workflows for fine-tuned model development
Translated ambiguous research objectives into sprint-ready backlogs with measurable KPIs
Improved data pipeline throughput ~30% through root cause analysis and re-sequencing
Served as primary point of contact between technical leads and C-Suite stakeholders
Client-side Agile PMO lead across ~10 distributed teams spanning India and California
Produced recurring executive dashboards tracking delivery KPIs and program portfolio progress
Drove 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency and 20% reduction in sprint carryover
Acted as escalation point for delivery risk, staffing issues, and cross-team blockers

before: AI transformation programs stall between strategy and execution — workstreams lack clear ownership
Executives don't know where things stand until a status meeting surfaces a surprise
Risks surface as emergencies rather than decisions made ahead of time
Client trust erodes when reporting lags behind reality
after: Clear milestone visibility across every active workstream
RAID-managed dependencies resolved before they become blockers
Executive reporting that anticipates questions before they're asked
A delivery culture where client leadership can focus on strategy, not status

the_ramp.deliverable: Review active client engagement portfolio, shadow two ongoing program status meetings, read existing project charters and RAID logs to establish risk posture — arrive with questions, not assumptions.
Own governance cadence for at least one active client engagement; begin producing status reporting in 66degrees format.
Produce first full executive status report; identify 2–3 process improvements in current delivery reporting structure; establish working relationships with three client-side stakeholders.
